_doc: Trust-signal ground-truth benchmark. Labels are hand-marked from the RENDERED page (screenshots + raw DOM evidence: headings, footer, short-text, logo walls, structural counts), INDEPENDENT of the detector — including cells where the detector is wrong. 1=present, 0=absent per (site x trust-type). The runner (run.ts) replays each capture, runs the real trust detector, and scores precision/recall/F1 vs these labels. CI coverage: the corpus/ captures AND the tracked fixtures/drift-survey/ captures are committed, so the trust-eval gate scores all 30 of them in CI; only the two fixtures/angel-sample/ captures (everlane, figma) are gitignored, so a local run scores all 32 (run.ts skips any capture not on disk). The drift-survey .gitignore rule guards against NEW large freezes being added by accident; the existing ones predate it and stay tracked. See README.md.

hubspot._note: committed corpus. Customer quote + logo wall; '299,000+ customers' (social proof); G2/award badges. Logos but no 'trusted by N companies' text.

linear._note: committed corpus. 2 testimonial quotes + an 8-logo inline-svg strip. social_proof_count CORRECTED 0->1: independently verified a visible first-party adoption claim 'Linear powers over 33,000 product teams' (the count sits in a <strong> at y~9208, deep below the fold, missed in the original above-fold labeling pass). Same claim class as hubspot/loom/dev-to.

hibob._note: committed corpus. 'Customer stories'; 8-logo strip; review stars; 'Join 5,000+ companies and 1,230,201 people using HiBob'.

supabase._note: community quote testimonials + CUSTOMER STORIES; 15-logo wall; SOC2/HIPAA/ISO in footer; 'Trusted by ... companies'. 104.4K is GitHub stars, not a customers stat.

vercel._note: 7-svg customer wall under 'Customers'. 'Custom SSL Certificates' is a product FEATURE, not a compliance cert.

loom._note: 6 blockquote testimonials; 12-logo wall; '400,000 companies choose Loom' (trusted_by + social_proof).

stripe._note: customer case studies (Hertz/URBN/Instacart); 36-svg logo carousel; '$1.9T in 2025','150K+ users'. No cert/checkout badge on homepage.

rei._note: 18 product star ratings; 'Returns & Exchanges'/'Return Policy' = guarantee. No customer-logo wall. The badge-path imgs are app-store, NOT review badges.

shopify-store-gymshark._note: 7-logo strip is the PAYMENT methods strip (visa/mc/paypal/klarna), NOT customer logos. 'Returns Policy' = guarantee.

patagonia._note: 'Ironclad Guarantee' + 'Returns & Exchanges'. The 5-img wall is Patagonia's OWN program icons, NOT customer logos.

warby-parker._note: 'Free 30-day returns' = guarantee. No logo wall, ratings, or other signals on this homepage capture.

verge._note: News site. Page-wide article-thumbnail scatter, ZERO marketing trust signals -> false-positive control.

airbnb._note: Thin SPA capture: listing cards w/ '4.96 out of 5 average rating'. Nothing else.

booking._note: Property review SCORE widgets '8.5 Very Good 3,339 reviews' (/10, split nodes); '300M+ reviews','2+ million properties'. Feature-icon wall != customer logos.

uber._note: App-download homepage. No trust signals -> false-positive control.

klarna._note: Trustpilot/5-star rating + review_badge; 70 payment imgs; 'Trusted by millions worldwide'; '119m users worldwide'. Several signals are carousel-hidden or split nodes -> known detector misses.

tradera._note: SE marketplace. Footer '4.7' app rating (bare number -> not safely catchable); 'Saker betalning' + 6 payment imgs. 15-img wall is product listings.

everlane._note: '30-Day Easy Returns' + footer 'RETURN POLICY' = guarantee. Minimal homepage.

trello._note: 3 blockquote testimonials w/ attribution. No logo wall on this capture. Proof is percentages, NOT counts.

lemonde._note: Le Monde news site. Zero marketing trust signals -> false-positive control.

intercom._note: 3 blockquote testimonials w/ attribution; 24-svg customer wall under 'Customers'. No cert/badge/social-count surfaced.

notion._note: blockquote testimonial; 17-logo wall; 'Trusted by 98% of the Forbes Cloud 100'; 'Over 100M users worldwide' (abbreviated); '#1 ... (G2)' G2 award badges. '#1 (G2)' is an award, not a star rating.

figma._note: 2 blockquote testimonials w/ name+role; 12-logo wall (airbnb/atlassian/dropbox/microsoft/nyt). Logos but no 'trusted by N' claim.

glossier._note: Minimalist beauty homepage. Footer 'Returns & exchanges' = guarantee. No logo wall; star clusters are decorative, no rating values shown.

ikea-se._note: SE. '4.4 utav 5 stjarnor (6014)' product rating; 'Oppet kop i 365 dagar' = guarantee; footer payment strip (visa/mc/amex/swish). Product/category walls != customer logos.

shopify-store-allbirds._note: Footer 'Returns/Exchanges' + 'Refund policy' = guarantee. Free-shipping copy is not a guarantee. Nothing else.

spotify._note: Thin web-player/consent-wall capture. No marketing trust signals -> false-positive control.

substack._note: Creator homepage; this capture surfaces only engagement counters, no clear quotes/stats -> labeled empty (false-positive control).

dev-to._note: 'DEV Community is a community of 3,958,285 amazing developers' = social_proof_count (tests the 'developers' unit). Otherwise an article feed -> controls the rest.

aftonbladet._note: SE tabloid news. Article-thumbnail lists, casino/ad badges. Zero marketing trust signals -> false-positive control.

spiegel._note: DE news (Der Spiegel). Article headlines only -> false-positive control.

github-blog._note: GitHub BLOG (not the product homepage). Post cards + a text Gartner-Leader mention (not a badge image). Nav tiles != customer logos -> controls.
